artifacts.content:
Hi Kaz,

Just grabbed five minutes in the crib room before the boys get back from smoko, thought I’d check in.

Didn’t sleep much again last night – maybe 3 hours all up. Same bloody Uruzgan 2004 nightmare with AJ. Woke up soaked, heart going a million miles an hour, ended up on the couch with you and Rusty at 3 am watching that stupid renovation show until I settled. I hate that you have to keep getting dragged into it, sitting up with me like that.

I keep thinking about how it all piles up – the panic in the Clyde car park since around ANZAC Day last year, those days in June when I rang Mark from the TransMetro depot car park saying I was “crook” because I was frozen in the car after that 14/08/05 IED flashback. Then February’s $8,600 stocktake stuff‑up with the pallet of phone accessories and the written warning from Mark on 19/02/24. No wonder I feel like my brain’s cooked half the time.

I’ve got that appointment with Dr Thompson at Riverside Family Medical, Suite 3, 128 Riverside Ave, on Thursday mid‑morning. I’m going to try to be honest about the 2–3 beers most nights turning into heavier stuff after late shifts, and how I’ve been more jumpy in the warehouse – like when that pallet slipped near me in the loading bay last July and I almost hit the deck.

If I’m being straight, part of me just wants him to write a medical certificate so I can wind my hours back without copping grief from TransMetro management. I know you want me to do the whole proper treatment thing, but right now I’m just knackered and want a bit of breathing space.

Anyway, your late shift tonight at Westmead – what time are you finishing? I’ll sort something easy for dinner, maybe defrost the spag bol. Text me if you want a lift.

Love,
Dave

Draft for GP – don’t forget this stuff

Appointment with Dr Michael Thompson – Riverside Family Medical Clinic, Parramatta – Thursday 10:30 am.

Things to bring up:
- Sleep: most nights only 3–4 hrs broken sleep. Nightmares 3–4 times a week, especially around the Tarin Kowt anniversary each September. That’s when I told Emily not to stay over at the unit on Anzac Parade because I’m up pacing the hallway at 3–4 am and yelling in my sleep.
- July 2023 weekend when Em was staying – panic attack + too many drinks, yelled at her and punched the bedroom door. That led to Sarah pushing for the Parramatta Family Court orders and limiting overnights to two weekends a month.
- October 2023 blow‑up with Karen – after a week of 2–3 hrs sleep a night, argument about money and the 3–4 beers every evening, and I put another hole in the bedroom door. Not proud of that.
- Work: Feb 2024 $8.6k stocktake error at TransMetro Clyde, written warning 19/2. Plus the PIP from Mark O’Neill on 3/5/24 after 17 sick days in 6 months (back pain + panic attacks).
- Pain: back and right knee – worse since that fall down Mum and Dad’s back stairs in Toongabbie on 4/9/23 carrying Dad’s toolbox. Physio Mark Ellison at Westmead Outpatients (report 20/9/23) said no more than 5–10 mins of stairs and no lifting over 15 kg, especially after late shifts.
- Medication: sertraline, meloxicam, panadol – and admit I’ve been taking Mum’s leftover Endone 5 mg on 3–4 nights a week since her Westmead knee replacement in March 2023 when pain and sleep are bad.

Mainly I want something short term – a few sessions with whoever he sends me to so I can say I’ve given it a go and then get on with things, not some never‑ending program.

Hi Sarah,

I’ve been meaning to write properly instead of just firing off quick texts about changeovers.

I know things have been tense since that July 2023 weekend at my place in Westmead when I’d been drinking too much and had the panic attack, yelled, and put my fist through the bedroom door while Em was there. I still feel sick thinking about her face. I understand why you went through Parramatta Family Court and tightened up the overnights to the two weekends a month.

Lately I’ve been trying to sort myself out a bit. I’m seeing Dr Sarah Nguyen, the psychiatrist Thompson referred me to, every few weeks. We’ve been working on grounding and breathing so I don’t go straight from zero to explosion. I’ve also been talking to her about the September Tarin Kowt anniversary stuff – how the nightmares ramp up and I ask you to swap weekends so Em doesn’t have to hear me shouting at 3 am.

I’m also trying to cut back on the booze. After late shifts at TransMetro in Clyde I’d got into a bad habit of coming home and smashing way too much Jim Beam to knock myself out. I scared myself in November driving back along Parramatta Road after one of those nights and only just scraping under the 0.05 on an RBT. I can’t risk that happening again, especially with Em in the car.

I’m not asking to change the orders right now. I just wanted you to know I’m actually doing some work on this, not just paying lip service. If you’re okay with it, I’d like to take Em to Westfield Parramatta next month to get some HSC supplies and maybe talk about uni. I’ll stick to the agreed times and keep you posted.

Thanks for reading,
Dave

Michael: You coming to Dan’s thing on Sunday? Lisa’s doing a BBQ.

Dave: Yeah, I’ll be there. Still feel like a goose about his 13th on 6 Aug when I had that panic attack at your place in Kellyville, yelled at you in front of the kids and necked four vodka shots in 20 minutes then bailed.

Michael: Water under the bridge mate. Just don’t disappear this time.

Dave: Trying. Been rough since that pallet slipped off the forklift in the loading bay at TransMetro on 18/7/24. Proper panic episode – thought it was another IED like Uruzgan 2005. My GP at Westmead Medical Centre, Dr Cartwright, wrote a letter on 22/7 saying no therapy stuff with explosion or gunfire recordings and that I should sit near the exit so I can get out if I get triggered.

Michael: Fair enough. How’s the heart stuff?

Dave: Still have to be careful. Remember that stress test at Westmead Cardiology on 9/5/22 when my BP shot up to 185/105 after 11 mins and I nearly blacked out? A/Prof Ng told me any new exercise has to start real slow, 10–15 min warm-up, no HIIT or incline, especially not at the Clyde work gym.

Michael: So no F45 then haha.

Dave: Exactly. Plus after that forklift near‑miss in Jan when I almost reversed into the rack because I was nodding off after back‑to‑back late shifts, my GP said no more sessions or appointments after 6 pm or straight off nights. Brain just doesn’t work.

Michael: Just look after yourself. Mum and Dad’s 55th at Toongabbie RSL next Oct – we want you there the whole time, no early exits.

Dave: That’s the plan. I’ll drive and stick to the light beers.

Stuff to remember for psych with Dr Sarah Nguyen – next Tuesday 11 am

1. Work
- Talk about how my world’s shrunk – cancelling pub catch-ups with the old Army boys in Parramatta because I’m wiped after shifts.
- The February 2024 stocktake mess at TransMetro Clyde: miscounted that high-value pallet of phone accessories, $8,600 out, written warning from Mark Reynolds on 19/2/24. Still feel sick thinking the next mistake could cost me the part-time supervisor role.
- Performance improvement plan from Mark O’Neill on 3/5/24 after 17 sick days in 6 months (back pain flare-ups + panic attacks in the Clyde car park and loading bay).

2. Home
- October 2023 argument with Karen at the unit in Westmead – money + drinking. Week of only 2–3 hours sleep a night, nightmares every time I closed my eyes. Snapped and punched a hole in the bedroom door. She said that was the line in the sand.
- How Karen sometimes has to sit up with me on the couch after the Afghanistan nightmares, especially the Uruzgan 2004 one when AJ was killed.

3. Emily
- July 2023 weekend – panic + heavy drinking, yelled and punched the door while she was staying. Led to Sarah pushing for the Parramatta Family Court orders. Want help working out how to rebuild trust.
- September pattern around the Tarin Kowt incident – tell Emily not to stay over those weekends because of the screaming and pacing.

4. Goals
- Would be good if we can work on sleep, cutting back the beers, and getting moving again without wrecking my back/knee. But honestly I don’t see myself keeping up with heaps of homework or exercise logs – I mostly just want somewhere to talk things through now and then without getting pushed too hard.

Event: Review with GP – Dr Michael Thompson
Date: 2024-07-22
Time: 10:45–11:30 am
Location: Riverside Family Medical Clinic, Suite 3, 128 Riverside Avenue, Parramatta NSW 2150

Notes:
- Update him on PTSD stuff since last visit: panic attacks at Clyde Station getting off the train for afternoon shifts, especially since ANZAC Day 2023. Mention those two days in June 2024 when I sat shaking in the TransMetro depot car park and rang Mark to say I was “sick” because I was stuck in a flashback to the 14/08/05 IED incident.
- Tell him I’ve started seeing Dr Sarah Nguyen regularly and using the breathing/grounding exercises in the car before going into busy places like Bunnings, Westfield Parramatta, or the warehouse.
- Sleep: still only 3–4 hours a night most weeks. Nightmares worse around the Tarin Kowt anniversary each September – still asking Sarah to swap Emily’s weekends so Em doesn’t hear me yelling at 3–4 am in the unit on Anzac Parade.
- Pain and mobility: back and right knee still bad since the fall down Mum and Dad’s back stairs in Toongabbie on 4/9/23. Physio Mark Ellison’s advice from 20/9/23 – no more than 5–10 minutes of stairs, no lifting over 15 kg, especially after late shifts.
- Cardio precautions: remind him about the Westmead Cardiology stress test on 9/5/22 when my BP spiked to 185/105 after 11 minutes and I nearly blacked out. A/Prof Ng said low-intensity only, 10–15 min warm-up, no unsupervised HIIT or incline at the Clyde work gym.
- Alcohol: admit I’ve been using 2–3 beers most nights, sometimes more after late shifts.

Bring: updated meds list, physio report, any letters from Dr Cartwright re triggers (explosion/gunfire sounds, need to sit near exit, allowed to leave if overwhelmed).

Hi HR,

I’m writing to follow up on the previous discussions about my duties and attendance at TransMetro Logistics in Clyde.

As you know, I’ve had a rough run health-wise over the past 18 months. On 4 September 2023 I fell down the back stairs at my parents’ place in Toongabbie while carrying my dad Peter’s old toolbox, which stirred up longstanding lower back issues and right knee osteoarthritis. The physiotherapist at Westmead Hospital Outpatient Physio, Mark Ellison, sent through a report dated 20 September 2023 recommending I avoid more than 5–10 minutes of continuous stair climbing and lifting over 15 kg, particularly when I’m fatigued after late shifts.

On top of that, I’ve been dealing with chronic PTSD. I’ve had several panic episodes at work – including one in the loading bay on 18 July 2024 when a pallet slipped off the forklift and crashed near me. My GP at Westmead Medical Centre, Dr Helen Cartwright, has advised that loud, sudden impact noises and anything resembling explosion/gunfire recordings are a major trigger. She’s asked that, where possible, I’m rostered away from the noisiest bays and that any training sessions using such material allow me to sit near an exit and step out if needed.

Earlier this year, on 15 January 2024, my supervisor John Reynolds spoke with me after I nearly reversed a forklift into a pallet rack following back-to-back late shifts. My GP has since recommended avoiding shifts that roll straight from nights into early mornings, and I’ve been trying to manage fatigue better to reduce any safety risk.

I’m committed to keeping my part-time supervisor role and pulling my weight. I’d appreciate a meeting to review my current restrictions and see if we can formalise some reasonable adjustments around lifting limits, stair use, and shift patterns so I can keep working safely and reliably.

Regards,
David Mercer
Warehouse Supervisor (Part-time)
TransMetro Logistics Pty Ltd

metadata.subject: Request to review duties and restrictions

artifacts.content:
From: David Mercer <d.mercer74@example.com>
To: Dr Sarah Nguyen <s.nguyen@riversidementalhealth.com>
Date: 9 February 2023
Subject: Follow-up after Westmead ED visit

Hi Sarah,

Just wanted to jot this down before I forget too many details and so we can talk it through properly at our next appointment.

On Friday 3 February 2023 I ended up at the Westmead Hospital emergency department after what I guess was a full-blown panic attack. Karen was on an afternoon shift at Westmead, so she actually met me there. The waiting room was packed and there was a couple at the front desk having a loud argument about triage and waiting times. As soon as their voices went up, my chest tightened and it felt like I was right back at Parramatta Local Court during the AVO stuff with Sarah in November 2021.

Ever since that domestic argument at our old place in Penrith that led to the AVO, I’ve noticed I get extremely anxious in crowded waiting rooms, especially if there are raised voices or couples arguing. This time it got bad enough that I couldn’t catch my breath, hands shaking, sweating, the whole lot.

The triage nurse at Westmead Hospital picked up pretty quickly that I wasn’t just “a bit stressed.” She moved me into a quieter side room away from the main entrance and the front desk. Once I was away from the noise, the grounding and breathing exercises we’ve practised started to kick in and I settled down after a while.

Can we add to my notes that, where possible, I should be seated in a quieter area and not near the main entrance in ED or clinic waiting rooms? It really seemed to make a difference.

Thanks,
David
Unit 7, 24 Anzac Parade
Westmead NSW 2145

[Signal Chat – Dave & Mick]

Dave (07:42):
Hey mate, sorry I’ve been a ghost. Been meaning to catch up at the pub in Parra but the head’s been a bit cooked.

Mick (07:44):
All good cobber. Thought you’d gone soft on us. You alright or just hiding from my shout? 😂

Dave (07:49):
Bit of both. You remember that AVO mess with Sarah back in November 2021 at the old place in Penrith? Ever since the court hearing at Parramatta Local Court I’ve been pretty jumpy in crowded spots, especially if people start yelling.

Dave (07:51):
Had a shocker at Westmead Hospital emergency department on 3 Feb 2023. Waiting room was packed, some couple at the front desk started going off at the receptionist and I just lost it. Heart racing, couldn’t breathe, thought I was going to hit the deck.

Dave (07:53):
Triage nurse clocked it was more than just being cranky about the wait and stuck me in a little side room away from the main entrance. Helped heaps once it was quiet, but it rattled me. Feels like my body thinks I’m back in that courtroom every time voices go up.

Mick (07:58):
Shit, mate. That sounds rough. Wish you’d yelled out sooner. We can ditch the pub if it’s too much. Coffee somewhere quiet in Parra or Toony instead?

Dave (08:01):
Yeah, that’d be better. Somewhere low-key, not too crowded. I’m trying to actually listen to the psych and avoid triggers instead of muscling through like we used to.

Mick (08:03):
Done. I’ll find a quiet café near Parra Park. Saturday arvo?

Dave (08:05):
Sounds good. Cheers, brother.

metadata.title: Chat with Mick re: panic in waiting rooms
